core_mechanism: a control-parameter-driven quantum phase transition/crossover arising from the competition between two opposing energy scales (an on-lattice interaction/coupling versus a kinetic tunneling scale), where tuning their ratio drives the system between qualitatively distinct collective states such as a delocalized/itinerant phase and a localized/frozen phase
brief_reason: The abstract frames every example - the Mott-Hubbard transition in optical lattices, strongly interacting low-dimensional gases, and the BCS-BEC crossover near Feshbach resonances - as strong-coupling many-body behavior controlled by tuning an interaction-to-kinetic-energy ratio across a threshold or crossover, which is the shared transferable machinery.
